entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A food legume grown as a field and/or backyard crop in over 82 countries.

entities.name: GENOTYPING
entities.description: The process of determining the genetic makeup of the pigeonpea composite collection.

entities.name: SSR MARKERS
entities.description: Simple Sequence Repeat markers used for genotyping pigeonpea.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: A major pigeonpea growing country with 3.2 million ha.

entities.description: A major pigeonpea growing country with 0.48 million ha.

entities.name: KENYA
entities.description: A major pigeonpea growing country with 0.15 million ha.

entities.name: MALAWI
entities.description: A major pigeonpea growing country with 0.12 million ha.

entities.name: VEGETARIAN PROTEIN
entities.description: A nutritional component provided by pigeonpea.

entities.name: SOIL ENRICHMENT
entities.description: The capacity of pigeonpea to act as a soil enricher.

entities.name: FODDER
entities.description: The use of pigeonpea as animal feed.

entities.name: FUEL WOOD
entities.description: The use of pigeonpea for fuel purposes.

entities.name: SOIL EROSION
entities.description: A soil degradation process that pigeonpea helps to arrest.

relationships.description: Genotyping is performed on pigeonpea composite collection
SSR markers are used as the tool for genotyping
Pigeonpea is a good source of vegetarian protein
Pigeonpea acts as a soil enricher
Pigeonpea is used as fuel wood
Pigeonpea is used for arresting soil erosion
